channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flytipped rubbish, 15th July
item.description:
Black bags full of rubbish and builders waste

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chapman's Lane, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 Forest commercial hire bikes obstructing the public footway at the junction of Newlands Park and Lennard Rd, by Penge East Station., 15th July
item.description:
3 Forest commercial hire bikes obstructing the public footway at the junction of Newlands Park and Lennard Rd, by Penge East Station. Seen 12.05pm Wednesday 15/7/26

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 226, Newlands Park, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large mound of tarmac in centre of road, 15th July
item.description:
Expanded tarmac has created a very large mound in the centre of the road. It has been there at least a few days
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Taunton Drive, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest and Lime commercial hire bikes continually being dumped on the public footway and obstructing the footway dropped kerb, tactile paved footway at the junction of Newlands Park and Venner Rd, by Penge East Station., 15th July
item.description:
Forest and Lime commercial hire bikes continually being dumped on the public footway and obstructing the footway dropped kerb, tactile paved footway at the junction of Newlands Park and Venner Rd, by Penge East Station. Seen 12.05pm Wednesday 15/7/26

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 210, Venner Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest and Lime commercial hire bikes continually being dumped on the public footway and obstructing the footway dropped kerb, tactile paved footway at the junction of Newlands Park and Venner Rd, by Penge East Station., 15th July
item.description:
Forest and Lime commercial hire bikes continually being dumped on the public footway and obstructing the footway dropped kerb, tactile paved footway at the junction of Newlands Park and Venner Rd, by Penge East Station. Seen 12.05pm Wednesday 15/7/26

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 210, Venner Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cars parked on pavement, 15th July
item.description:
I am a disabled & drive a mobility scooter. I have serious problems navigating Woodcroft as there are so many cars/vans parked either half or fully on the pavement. My mobility scooter is not made to ride on the road just the pavement. It can be quite scary when you are navigating crossing the road when you can't see whats coming. I do appreciate there is a really bad parking problem in Woodcroft but I'm really fed up with this no-one seems to care.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woodcroft, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car been left for weeks not moved, 15th July
item.description:
Car has been here for weeks not moved once. Checked tax and seen it is an untaxed car since 1st June 2026 Its hard enough to get a parking space on felspar close at the best of times let alone someone leaving their cars here

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Felspar Close, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken glass on road and footpath, 15th July
item.description:
Broken glass on the road and footpath. Will hurt dogs and burst tires. Also broken glass on the path in the park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 106, Abercorn Way, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bikes blocking access to Bonita Mews, 15th July
item.description:
This is a private road and e bikes should not be parked here. They are blocking parking access to residents. This is the fourth time I have had to report e bikes being poorly parked affecting residents. Voi have not changed anything, if anything they have added more bikes as can be seen by the actual pile of bikes inside the parking bay.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bonita Mews, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Brocken plastic / glass on toad, 15th July
item.description:
A coach was there this week with front smashed in and no front number plate. Now that coach has been removed, but debris with brocken items has been left on the road on the Wheathill House side, which could damage tyres.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Kids are fishing in the pond , and throwing dirt in, 15th July
item.description:
Kids are fishing in the pond , and throwing dirt in

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Clapton Common, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement right of way obstruction, 15th July
item.description:
1. Outside is industrial (private) bin on public pathway, it is being used by the householder as part of their business in the property food related business It is an obstruction for mobility and wheelchairs and prams who cannot pass and elderly who have to navigate aroubd the tree then the bin, also on the side which people need to access the pharmacy. They cannot cross over due to parking. 2. Further down a couple of doors is a overgrown hedge in another property which you have to bend to walk under before you get tobthe above obstruction.. There are also delivery bikes chained tobthe lampost judt after the bin causing mote obstruction. This bin is not Greenwich council but there own private bin as i have seen it be removed and emptying by a private company. This is causing a lot of obstruction to residents locally who use the pharmacy and have to go via another rd to get there which us hazardous as well.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Burrage Place, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping on the grass verge outside No. 37 Merewood Road, 15th July
item.description:
The waste has been illegally deposited on public land, creating an eyesore and a potential environmental and safety hazard. As a local resident, I am concerned that if it is not removed promptly, additional rubbish may be dumped in the same location.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 35, Merewood Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blood on floor, 15th July
item.description:
Stabbing happened last friday! The house door still has hand blood prints and there is blood all down the drive and then puddles of blood on public pavements! Families and old people should not have to see this on a daily basis!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dorothy Evans Close, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees, 15th July
item.description:
Tnt inform that the tree at the back of the sheltered house requires water. The bag is empty. Mrs R De Senneville Flat 1, Monro House, 116 Fitzjohn's Avenue, London, NW3 6NU 0207 435 2031
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 116, Fitzjohn's Avenue, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging branches, 15th July
item.description:
The branches are so low it is difficult to walk along the pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glanfield Road, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The notice board is damaged and defaced, 15th July
item.description:
This notice board, on the west side of the main lake in sight of the rose garden, has been damaged and defaced for over a year. It is an eye sore and needs to be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Manor Way, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grey vw not moved in 3 weeks, 15th July
item.description:
Car not moved in 3 weeks...parked long way from kerb
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbey Crescent, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: E-bikes stopping disable people to do normal life, 15th July
item.description:
There were so many bikes that my friend on wheelchair couldn’t use the crosswalk. They should be parked somewhere out of crossing points as disable people can not go through. Even for me was hard to move around the bikes to try and cross. Ridiculous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 167, Piccadilly, Mayfair, City of Westminster
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stolen bins outside house for weeks, 15th July
item.description:
These bins have remained outside 10 Hadrian Street for weeks, despite Hadrian street not having big plastic bins. Nobody else puts bins out, just bin bags the morning of bin day. These bins also say “Banning Street” on them, which is a nearby road, so have clearly been stolen from there. They remain outside and have been there for weeks/months. It is not pleasant and is blocking a public pathway. The same house also had a sofa on the path for months (I can see that has been listed previously in a report too).

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hadrian Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet
